criteria.name: Narrative Structure
criteria.description: Does the story follow a clear arc (context → problem → evidence → resolution)?

scores.1: No narrative structure - data dump without story
scores.2: Weak structure - some story elements but disorganized
scores.3: Adequate structure - recognizable arc with some gaps
scores.4: Good structure - complete arc with clear transitions
scores.5: Excellent structure - compelling arc with hook, build, climax, and resolution

criteria.name: Opening Effectiveness
criteria.description: Does the opening create interest and establish stakes?

scores.1: No hook - starts with generic data description
scores.2: Weak opening - some context but no compelling hook
scores.3: Adequate opening - establishes topic but could be more engaging
scores.4: Good opening - clear hook with human impact or surprising finding
scores.5: Excellent opening - immediately compelling, establishes stakes and curiosity

criteria.name: Annotation Quality
criteria.description: Do annotations guide interpretation without cluttering?

scores.4: Good annotations - key insights, events, and patterns clearly marked
scores.5: Excellent annotations - precise, well-placed, guiding reader through each insight

criteria.name: Pattern Appropriateness
criteria.description: Is the storytelling pattern (template and visual pattern) well-matched to the content?

scores.1: Wrong pattern - structure contradicts the data message
scores.2: Poor fit - pattern creates confusion or extra cognitive load
scores.3: Acceptable fit - pattern works but not optimal
scores.4: Good fit - pattern matches data type and audience well
scores.5: Optimal fit - pattern perfectly serves the narrative goal and audience

criteria.name: Context and Framing
criteria.description: Is sufficient context provided (baselines, comparisons, denominators) with balanced framing?

scores.1: No context - numbers without baselines or comparisons
scores.2: Minimal context - some reference points but key comparisons missing
scores.4: Good context - historical, peer, and benchmark comparisons with balanced framing
scores.5: Excellent context - comprehensive baselines, multiple frames offered, limitations noted

criteria.name: Self-Containedness
criteria.description: Can the story be understood without external knowledge or explanation?

scores.1: Requires extensive external knowledge - unintelligible alone
scores.3: Mostly self-contained with some assumptions about audience
scores.5: Fully self-contained - terminology defined, sources cited, methodology noted

criteria.name: Takeaway Clarity
criteria.description: Is the main insight clearly stated and supported by the evidence shown?

scores.1: No clear takeaway - reader left to guess the point
scores.2: Vague takeaway - insight implied but not stated
scores.3: Adequate takeaway - insight stated but could be clearer
scores.4: Clear takeaway - main insight prominently stated and well-supported
scores.5: Compelling takeaway - insight unmistakable, supported by evidence, with implications stated

passing_note: Average score must be >= 3.5 for story to be considered effective. Scores below 3 in Narrative Structure or Takeaway Clarity require revision before publication.
